ctx.name: Carlos
ctx.notes: For Carlos, this performance conversation needs to make the quality bar concrete and usable. I want to understand whether prior feedback translated into behavior changes or stayed abstract. I also need to hear how he defines good work right now and where he feels blocked in growth. Without that shared baseline, feedback can feel subjective and demotivating. My goal is to align on specific expectations, examples, and one near-term development focus he can practice immediately.

focusPoints.label: Concrete quality bar and what “good” means
focusPoints.reason: Whether prior feedback translated into behavior changes or stayed abstract, and what he uses as the definition of good work right now.

focusPoints.label: Feedback landing — both directions
focusPoints.reason: Whether the feedback he’s receiving is specific enough to act on, and whether he’s able to give you the same level of clarity on what’s working or not.

focusPoints.label: Near-term growth focus and practice plan
focusPoints.reason: What he feels blocked on in growth, and which one skill or workflow change he can practice immediately with clear examples and a short feedback loop.

focusPoints.label: Reliability on commitments and early flags
focusPoints.reason: For a Junior in Performance & feedback, predictability is a fast way to make feedback feel fair: commitments, early slippage signals, and closing the loop on what was agreed.

transcript.question: Before I share my view, how do you think the last stretch has gone?
transcript.answer: I can name wins, but I am not always sure they match the team bar.

transcript.question: You said 'team bar' — what feels unclear about it right now?
transcript.answer: Feedback has helped, though I still miss consistency under time pressure.

transcript.question: Under time pressure, what part slips first for you?
transcript.answer: I sometimes focus on polish and lose sight of the core message.

transcript.question: When you say a brand design is good, what tells you it's good?
transcript.answer: I want clearer examples of what excellent junior-level work looks like here.

transcript.question: What examples of excellent junior-level work would make the bar clearer for you?
transcript.answer: When reviews are broad, I am not sure what to change first.

transcript.question: Where do my expectations for your work feel different from yours right now?
transcript.answer: I would like to grow faster in concept rationale, not just execution speed.

transcript.question: Concept rationale — what does stronger work there look like to you?
transcript.answer: A tighter loop on one skill at a time would help me improve.

transcript.question: What is getting in the way of the next skill you want to build?
transcript.answer: If we align on concrete criteria, I can work against them confidently.

transcript.question: What's the first concrete change you want to make before we meet again?
